simulations.title: Color Mixer
simulations.description: Mix RGB light sources to explore additive color mixing.

simulations.title: Color Mixing Game
simulations.description: Test your color mixing skills in this interactive game.

simulations.title: RGB Channel Separator
simulations.description: Separate images into red, green, and blue channels to understand color addition.

simulations.title: Fire Tower
simulations.description: Interactive fire tower simulation exploring light and visibility.

simulations.title: Shadow Puppet Show
simulations.description: Create shadow puppets and explore how shadows form.

simulations.title: Shadow Demonstration
simulations.description: Explore how shadows change with light position.

simulations.title: Shadow Demonstration - Point Light
simulations.description: See how point light sources create different shadow effects.

simulations.title: Table Shadow Analysis
simulations.description: Analyze shadow geometry with adjustable lines and intersections.

simulations.title: Light Ray Simulation
simulations.description: Trace light rays through different media and surfaces.

simulations.title: Sun Rays and Block Tower 1
simulations.description: Explore how sunlight creates shadows with block structures.

simulations.title: Sun Rays and Block Tower 2
simulations.description: Extended sun ray simulation with different configurations.

simulations.title: Sun Rays and Block Tower 3
simulations.description: Advanced sun ray and shadow exploration.

simulations.title: Sun Rays and Block Tower 4
simulations.description: Final variation of sun ray shadow demonstrations.

simulations.title: Sun Getting Larger
simulations.description: Visualize what happens as the Sun's apparent size changes.

simulations.title: Moon Getting Farther
simulations.description: Explore how the Moon's distance affects its appearance.

simulations.title: Loaded String Simulation
simulations.description: Pluck a virtual string and observe wave motion.

simulations.title: Loaded String with Graph
simulations.description: String simulation with real-time position graph and timer.

simulations.title: Loaded String - Separated View
simulations.description: String simulation with separated graph display.

simulations.title: Sound Wave Propagation
simulations.description: Visualize how sound travels from source through medium to receiver.

simulations.title: Sound Visualizer
simulations.description: Capture and visualize sound waves from your microphone.

simulations.title: Vibration Position vs Time
simulations.description: Graph displacement over time for a single vibrating point.

simulations.title: Vibration Position-Time (Canvas)
simulations.description: Canvas-based vibration visualization.

simulations.title: Combined Waves
simulations.description: See what happens when two waves combine together.

simulations.title: Wave Superposition
simulations.description: Explore constructive and destructive interference when waves meet.

simulations.title: Wave Beating (Adjustable)
simulations.description: Adjust frequencies to explore the beating phenomenon.

simulations.title: Wave Beating (On/Off)
simulations.description: Toggle waves on and off to observe beating effects.

simulations.title: Displacement vs Time Waveform
simulations.description: Single wave displacement-time simulation.

simulations.title: Sound Wave Simulation
simulations.description: General sound wave simulation and visualization.

simulations.title: Speed of Sound Measurement
simulations.description: Newton's method for measuring the speed of sound.

simulations.title: Random Walk - Basic
simulations.description: Simple random walk simulation to explore probabilistic motion.

simulations.title: Random Walk - Interactive
simulations.description: Interactive random walk with adjustable parameters.

simulations.title: Random Walk - Time-Limited
simulations.description: Run time-limited random walk experiments and analyze results.

simulations.title: Two Microphones
simulations.description: Compare sound detection with two microphone positions.

simulations.title: Depth Perception – Quarter
simulations.description: See how two eyes perceive depth by dragging a quarter between two illustrated eyes and watching the sight lines converge. Illustrates binocular parallax as the basis of depth perception.

simulations.title: Depth Perception – Quarter
simulations.description: See how two eyes perceive depth by dragging a quarter between two illustrated eyes and watching the sight lines converge, with instructional text explaining how binocular parallax works.

simulations.title: Depth Perception – Quarter
simulations.description: See how two eyes perceive depth by dragging a quarter between two illustrated eyes and watching the sight lines converge. A taller variant that adds a mirror at the scene bottom.

simulations.title: Depth Perception – Quarter
simulations.description: Drag a quarter around the scene and observe how the two sight lines from two eyes change angle to reveal the coin's distance. Explores the geometry of binocular depth cues.

simulations.title: Depth Perception – Quarter
simulations.description: Drag a quarter and observe how the crossing angle of two sight lines changes with distance. A compact iframe-embedded version of the binocular parallax visualization.

simulations.title: Tilt Mirror
simulations.description: Drag a mirror on a table and tilt it to steer the reflection of a coin toward an eye. Shows how the angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection and how mirror tilt redirects light.

simulations.title: Corner Reflector
simulations.description: Draw light rays in a 90-degree corner reflector and watch them bounce off both mirrors. Demonstrates why a corner reflector sends light back in the direction it came from, regardless of entry angle.

simulations.title: Corner Reflector – Mirror Challenge
simulations.description: Rotate and flip a corner reflector and submit your answer to see whether a light ray returns to the source. Explores how parity (left-right flip) is related to an odd number of reflections.

simulations.title: Depth Perception
simulations.description: Click to draw sight lines from two eyes to dots at different depths. Reveals how the crossing angle of two lines of sight encodes distance.

simulations.title: Noise Cancellation – 1 Component
simulations.description: Adjust a single cancellation wave to perfectly cancel a noise signal and reduce the combined waveform to zero. Introduces destructive interference and active noise cancellation with one frequency component.

simulations.title: Noise Cancellation – 2 Components
simulations.description: Adjust amplitude and phase sliders for two frequency components to cancel a complex noise waveform. Builds on the one-component version by requiring students to cancel two simultaneous frequencies.

simulations.title: Noise Cancellation – 3 Components
simulations.description: Tune three separate cancellation waves to eliminate a three-component noise signal using destructive interference. Extends active noise-cancellation concepts to more complex real-world sound spectra.

simulations.title: Arrow Pair – Drag & Rotate
simulations.description: Drag, rotate, and flip two arrows on a canvas to match orientations. Supports exploring how mirror reflections change handedness compared with rotations.

simulations.title: Corner Reflector – Mirror Challenge 2
simulations.description: Rotate and flip a corner reflector challenge to decide whether a reflected arrow matches the original or is flipped. Focuses on how an even or odd number of reflections determines parity.

simulations.title: Corner Reflector – Second Reflection Visualization
simulations.description: Static diagram showing an arrow object, its first reflection in one mirror, and the final double-reflected image in a 90-degree corner reflector. Visualizes how each reflection flips handedness.

simulations.title: Corner Reflector – Reflection Visualization
simulations.description: Animated diagram of a single reflection in one mirror of a corner reflector, showing the object and its flipped image. Clarifies how one reflection reverses orientation.

simulations.title: Rainbow Overhead – Drag & Rotate
simulations.description: Drag and rotate two overhead views of a rainbow to compare their orientations. Used to reason about symmetry and how a rainbow's arc appears relative to the sun-antisolar axis.

simulations.title: String Tension & Frequency
simulations.description: Drag weight blocks onto a string over a pulley to change tension, then pluck the string and read the fundamental frequency. Demonstrates how tension affects pitch via f = (1/2L)√(T/μ).

simulations.title: Wave Superposition Simulation
simulations.description: Send two pulses of chosen shape and height toward each other on a string and watch them pass through each other. Shows the principle of superposition: the net displacement equals the sum of the individual waves.

simulations.title: Angle of Reflection Game
simulations.description: Look at a reflected ray and estimate the angle of incidence to earn a streak. Builds fluency with the law of reflection through rapid-fire practice.

simulations.title: Noise Cancellation – 2 Components (v4)
simulations.description: Adjust amplitude and phase for two frequency components to reduce a combined waveform to silence. An alternate UI version of the two-component noise cancellation puzzle.

simulations.title: Cylindrical Anamorphic Art Generator
simulations.description: Upload an image and see how it would appear distorted when reflected in a cylindrical mirror. A cylindrical anamorphic art generator that applies the optics of curved mirrors.

simulations.title: Doppler Effect – Flyby
simulations.description: Watch a moving sound source fly past an observer and count how many wave crests arrive per second. Illustrates the Doppler effect by showing compressed wavefronts ahead of the source and stretched ones behind.

simulations.title: Doppler Effect Simulation
simulations.description: Move an observer toward or away from a stationary sound source and observe how the counted frequency changes. Demonstrates the observer-motion case of the Doppler effect.

simulations.title: Doppler Effect – Moving Source
simulations.description: Adjust the speed of a moving sound source and observe the wavefront pattern around it. Shows how source speed compresses wavefronts in the forward direction and stretches them behind.

simulations.title: Little Mirrors
simulations.description: Place small mirrors to steer a light cone to a target screen. Students discover reflection angles and how to chain mirrors to redirect a beam.

simulations.title: Cyclops Mirrors
simulations.description: See how a cyclops character views its own face via a flat mirror and a convex cylindrical mirror. Compares image location and field of view for flat versus curved mirrors.

simulations.title: Cylindrical Anamorphic Art Generator (with Observer Eye)
simulations.description: Cylindrical anamorphic art generator with a movable observer eye to preview how the corrected image looks from the designated viewpoint. Connects the distorted flat drawing to the recovered image seen in the cylinder.

simulations.title: Cyclops Mirrors 2
simulations.description: A cyclops views itself in a cylindrical mirror; adjust the mirror diameter to change the image. Shows how the radius of curvature of a convex mirror affects apparent image size and position.

simulations.title: Concave Mirror
simulations.description: Slide a control to change the radius of curvature R of a concave mirror and see the focal length f = R/2 update live. Builds intuition for the geometric relationship between mirror shape and focal point.

simulations.title: Little Mirrors (Small)
simulations.description: A compact version of the mirror-steering puzzle where students place mirrors to redirect a light cone to a screen. Practices reflection angles in a smaller layout.

simulations.title: Concave Mirror – Ray Diagram
simulations.description: Shows how a light cone cast by an object changes size as the object moves relative to a concave mirror. Students discover the relationship between object distance and projected image size.

simulations.title: Concave Mirror – Radius & Focal Length
simulations.description: Static diagram of the Michelson speed-of-light setup showing the light path and octagonal mirror. Serves as a labeled reference diagram for why measuring round-trip time requires an equation.

simulations.title: Concave Mirror – Draggable Cone
simulations.description: Shows how a light cone cast by an ice cream cone changes size as the object moves relative to a concave mirror. Students discover the relationship between object distance and image size.

simulations.title: Telescope Mirror Types
simulations.description: Switch among parabolic, spherical, elliptical, and hyperbolic mirror shapes while adding rays to see how each conic section focuses parallel light. Reveals why parabolic mirrors are ideal for telescopes.

simulations.title: Concave Mirror
simulations.description: Add light rays and circular wavefronts around an interactive concave mirror, then zoom and pan to see where they converge. Provides a free-form ray-tracing sandbox for a concave mirror.

simulations.title: Concave Mirror – Draggable Cone
simulations.description: Drag an ice cream cone toward a concave mirror to watch the image flip and magnify as the object passes through the focal point. Shows how image type and magnification depend on object distance relative to f.

simulations.title: String Length & Frequency
simulations.description: Drag a hanging weight to shorten or lengthen a vibrating string, then pluck it to hear the resulting frequency. Demonstrates how string length controls pitch at fixed tension.

simulations.title: Telescope Mirror Comparison
simulations.description: A 2×2 grid comparing how parabolic, spherical, elliptical, and hyperbolic mirrors focus parallel rays. Provides a side-by-side comparison of conic mirror aberrations.

simulations.title: Test Iframe
simulations.description: An iframe test page that embeds a shadow-puppet simulation. Used for development and layout testing.

simulations.title: Measure the Speed of Light
simulations.description: Drag equipment onto two mountain peaks to recreate Michelson's 1926 experiment and measure the speed of light. Students send a light pulse, adjust mirror spin rate, and calculate c.

simulations.title: Globe Distance Measurer
simulations.description: Click two cities on an interactive 3D globe to measure the great-circle distance between them. Supports lessons on Eratosthenes's method and the geometry of Earth's surface.

simulations.title: Michelson Speed of Light Experiment
simulations.description: Spin the octagonal mirror in the Michelson apparatus and see whether the return beam hits the observer's eye. Focuses on the discrete-face geometry of the rotating mirror.

simulations.title: Michelson Speed of Light Experiment
simulations.description: Set the spin rate of the Michelson octagonal mirror in revolutions per second and watch the reflected light beam align or miss the observer. Connects mirror angular speed to the transit time of light over 35 km.

simulations.title: Foucault Speed of Light Experiment (1850)
simulations.description: Simulate Foucault's 1850 rotating-mirror experiment; adjust rotation or spin rate to steer the return beam onto the observer. Shows how a small angular deflection encodes the round-trip travel time of light.

simulations.title: Foucault Speed of Light — Simplified
simulations.description: A streamlined version of Foucault's rotating-mirror light-speed experiment. Helps students find the spin speed that steers the reflected beam back to the observer.

simulations.title: Sound Pressure Meter
simulations.description: A sound pressure meter visualization with a draggable microphone that reads off the pressure level at different distances from a source. Illustrates how sound pressure decreases with distance.

simulations.title: Mario Mirror Rotation
simulations.description: Rotate a familiar sprite image at adjustable speed to explore how a reflected image in a rotating mirror changes. Used to build intuition for image rotation in the Michelson experiment.

simulations.title: Foucault Speed of Light — Rotate
simulations.description: Compact version of the Foucault mirror apparatus focused on the rotation phase; adjust the deflection angle and see where the return ray lands.

simulations.title: Foucault Speed of Light — Spin
simulations.description: Compact spinning-mirror sub-simulation for the Foucault experiment; adjust spin speed in steps to align the return beam with the observer. Isolates the relationship between spin rate and angular beam deflection.

simulations.title: Foucault Speed of Light — Water Tube
simulations.description: Foucault spinning-mirror simulation with an optional water tube that slows light, recreating Fizeau's 1851 water experiment. Demonstrates that light travels slower in water than in air.

simulations.title: Anechoic Wedge Ray Reflection
simulations.description: Drag a sound source and aim point to fire rays into a row of anechoic foam wedges and count reflections. Shows how wedge-shaped absorbers trap sound through multiple bounces, explaining why anechoic chambers are nearly silent.

simulations.title: Slide Whistle
simulations.description: Drag the plug of an animated slide whistle to change the air column length and watch the frequency and standing-wave diagram update. Connects pipe length to pitch via the quarter-wave resonance formula.

simulations.title: Coffee Cup and Camera
simulations.description: Drag a camera up and down alongside a coffee cup containing a coin; light rays show what the camera can or cannot see. Explores how viewing angle determines whether a submerged object is visible.

simulations.title: Coffee Cup and Camera
simulations.description: Draw refracted rays from a coin in a water-filled cup to a camera at different heights. Demonstrates apparent-depth refraction and why a coin looks shallower when viewed through water.

simulations.title: Michelson Experiment - Setup Diagram
simulations.description: Static diagram of Michelson's 1926 light-speed apparatus, showing the octagonal mirror, distant reflector, and observer positions. Provides a reference layout for understanding the geometry before running the interactive experiment.

simulations.title: Michelson Experiment - Rotated 1°
simulations.description: Static diagram of the Michelson apparatus with the octagonal mirror rotated 1 degree, showing that the return beam misses the observer. Illustrates the angular sensitivity that makes the experiment work.

simulations.title: Standing Waves on a String
simulations.description: Slide a frequency control to drive a string and watch standing waves appear when the driver hits a resonant frequency. Shows how harmonics arise and how string amplitude spikes sharply at integer multiples of the fundamental.

simulations.title: Michelson Speed of Light Experiment
simulations.description: Interactive Michelson apparatus showing the full light path; spin the octagonal mirror and read off the computed speed of light. Includes a measurement readout so students can derive c from timing data.

simulations.title: Standing Waves in a C Bugle
simulations.description: Select harmonics on a bugle-shaped open tube and see the standing wave pattern, frequency, and wavelength update. Connects instrument geometry to the harmonic series through the formula for open-ended pipe resonance.

simulations.title: Wave Beating — Adjustable
simulations.description: Adjust the frequencies of two waves independently and observe their superposition beating at a rate equal to the frequency difference. Lets students discover the beat-frequency formula by experiment.

simulations.title: Wave Beating Simulation
simulations.description: Toggle each of two slightly different-frequency waves on and off while watching the combined waveform. Makes the origin of beats audible and visual by switching individual components.

simulations.title: Two-Speaker 1D Interference
simulations.description: Drag two speakers along a line and watch the superposition of their 1D waves; a bar at the right shows whether the listener hears constructive or destructive interference.

simulations.title: Two-Speaker Interference
simulations.description: Two speakers emit circular waves whose 2D interference pattern fills the screen; adjust frequency and toggle individual sources. Shows constructive and destructive fringes in the classic two-source pattern.

simulations.title: Refraction: Book, Water Container, Camera
simulations.description: Adjust sliders for angle of incidence and refractive index to see a ray bend at a glass-water interface, with live angle and speed data. A quantitative refraction explorer demonstrating Snell's law.

simulations.title: Mirror Shortest Path
simulations.description: Drag a light source, observer, and reflection point along a mirror to see total path length change. Demonstrates Fermat's principle of least time for mirrors.

simulations.title: Slide Word
simulations.description: Type a word and choose a symmetry operation (flip, rotate, reflect) to see how letter shapes transform. A visual tool for exploring how mirror reflections and rotations treat written text differently.

simulations.title: Refraction GIF Generator
simulations.description: A still reference diagram of the refraction setup showing rays bending at an air-water interface. Provides a GIF-compatible image of the book-in-water refraction demonstration.

simulations.title: Binary Orbits
simulations.description: Two stars orbit their common center of mass while emitting sound; Doppler-shifted crests mark the approaching and receding phases. Connects binary star orbital motion to the periodic frequency shifts used to detect them.

simulations.title: Fiber Refraction
simulations.description: Set the glass index of refraction and watch rays undergo total internal reflection inside each fiber bundle. Shows the principle behind optical fiber light transmission.

simulations.title: Refraction at Water-Air Interface
simulations.description: Drag a point in water and an eye in air; a refracted ray traces Snell's law between them showing where the apparent image appears. Demonstrates apparent depth and the effect of oblique refraction.

simulations.title: Doppler Effect – Flyby (Locked)
simulations.description: A locked-speed Doppler flyby: watch a source fly past at a fixed speed and count wavefronts to compare approach and recession frequencies. Designed for structured data collection.

simulations.title: Doppler Effect – Moving Observer (Locked)
simulations.description: Locked-speed Doppler simulation for a moving observer; the source is stationary while the observer moves at a fixed speed. Isolates the observer-motion Doppler formula for structured comparison.

simulations.title: Doppler Effect – Both Moving
simulations.description: Set independent sliders for both source and observer speeds and direction, then watch the resulting Doppler-shifted wavefronts. Explores the combined Doppler formula when neither source nor observer is stationary.

simulations.title: Prism — Minimum Deviation
simulations.description: Adjust apex angle, refractive index, and incidence angle of a prism to trace a refracted ray and find minimum deviation. Demonstrates why minimum deviation yields the most accurate index measurement.

simulations.title: Refraction Game
simulations.description: A light ray hits a glass interface — click which of two candidate refracted rays is correct. A streak game that drills the rule 'denser material, closer to normal' through rapid randomized problems.

simulations.title: Refraction Game (with normal)
simulations.description: Same choose-the-correct-refracted-ray game but with the surface normal drawn. Adds the explicit normal line to help students measure angles and apply Snell's law visually.

simulations.title: Ocean Wave Dispersion
simulations.description: Trigger a storm and watch wave packets of different wavelengths travel at different speeds, separating over time into a swell. Demonstrates ocean wave dispersion, where longer waves outrun shorter ones.

simulations.title: Wave Speed Comparison
simulations.description: A hammer strikes a grid of particles and two wave pulses travel at different speeds — one compressional (P) and one transverse (S). Shows side-by-side why P-waves arrive before S-waves in seismology.

simulations.title: Globe Trilateration
simulations.description: Draw distance circles on a globe from three seismic stations to locate an earthquake epicenter. Introduces trilateration as the technique GPS and seismometers use to find a source from arrival-time data.

simulations.title: S-Wave Shadow Zone
simulations.description: Adjust the Earth's core radius and watch S-wave paths refract or terminate at the liquid core boundary, shading the shadow zone. Explains why S-waves cannot pass through Earth's liquid outer core.

simulations.title: P-Wave Shadow Zone
simulations.description: Slide the inner solid core radius to see how P-waves refract through the outer and inner cores. Reveals Earth's internal layered structure from seismic wave paths.

simulations.title: Globe Trilateration
simulations.description: A more detailed globe trilateration puzzle with labeled seismic stations and location markers. Extends the first trilateration activity with additional real-world station data.

simulations.title: Seafloor Sound Refraction
simulations.description: Adjust wavelength, incidence angle, and seafloor sound speed to watch waves refract at the water-sediment boundary. Demonstrates how sonar waves bend when sound travels faster in the seafloor than in water.

simulations.title: Refraction: Apparent Depth
simulations.description: Drag two eyes above a water surface and trace refracted rays from a dot on the bottom; the intersection shows where the dot appears. Explores how viewing angle changes the perceived position of a submerged object.

simulations.title: Sound Refraction Simulation
simulations.description: Slide a temperature gradient control and watch sound rays curve upward or downward correspondingly. Illustrates atmospheric refraction of sound and why temperature inversions cause sound to travel farther.

simulations.title: Doppler Effect – Speed %
simulations.description: A moving sound source with speed expressed as a percentage of the speed of sound; adjust the slider and watch wavefront spacing change. Connects source-speed fraction to the Doppler frequency ratio.

simulations.title: Huygens' Principle
simulations.description: Draw a wavefront on a canvas, then animate Huygens' construction to watch each point emit a secondary wavelet and the next wavefront emerge. Directly visualizes Huygens' principle of wave propagation.

simulations.title: Huygens' Principle — Air/Water
simulations.description: Draw a wavefront that crosses an air-water boundary, then animate Huygens' construction to see wavelets shrink on the water side and the wavefront bend. Explains refraction at an interface through Huygens' principle.

simulations.title: Pipe End Correction
simulations.description: New simulation - description pending.

simulations.title: Sun Reflection off Glass Skyscraper
simulations.description: New simulation - description pending.
